steps.title: Use the height formula
steps.content: For an equilateral triangle with side length $s$, the height is found by splitting the triangle into two $30$-$60$-$90$ triangles. The height is the longer leg.

steps.title: Solve for the side length
steps.content: Substitute the given height and isolate $s$. First multiply both sides by $2$, then divide by $\sqrt{3}$: $$42\sqrt{3}=\frac{s\sqrt{3}}{2}$$ $$84\sqrt{3}=s\sqrt{3}$$ $$s=84$$ The answer is B.
